channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The draw for the FIFA’s revamped Club World Cup is coming up. Here’s what to know
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Soccer’s biggest ever global club tournament is coming to the United States next year and the 32-team group stage will be drawn Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matt Eberflus' dismissal from Bears came after 's--- show' postgame locker room speech: report
item.description: The Chicago Bears moved on from Matt Eberflus as head coach, but it didn't come before a tense locker room moment following yet another head-scratching loss for the group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liverpool's lead cut in Premier League and Man City ends slump. Chelsea and Arsenal win
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Liverpool’s lead at the top of the Premier League was cut to seven points after a thrilling 3-3 draw with Newcastle on Wednesday. Chelsea moved up to second by thrashing last-place Southampton 5-1, while Arsenal is third after a 2-0 win over Manchester United.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ravens suspend Diontae Johnson 1 game after refusing to play vs Eagles
item.description: The Baltimore Ravens announced they have suspended wide receiver Diontae Johnson one game for refusing to play against the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Commanders' Austin Ekeler says he suffered memory loss after second concussion this season
item.description: Washington Commanders running back Austin Ekeler was placed on injured reserve this past weekend after he sustained his second concussion in a span of just two months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trevor Lawrence placed on injured reserve, likely ending his season after dangerous hit
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars have placed Trevor Lawrence on injured reserve, likely ending his season after the hit he took earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jaguars coach Doug Pederson defends his team's reaction to illegal hit on QB Trevor Lawrence
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Jacksonville Jaguars coach Doug Pederson has no problem with how his team reacted to the illegal hit on quarterback Trevor Lawrence, who was placed on injured reserve.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Jose Sharks have one representative at 4 Nations Face-Off
item.description: San Jose Sharks center Mikael Granlund chosen, as expected, to play for Finland at the 4 Nations Face-Off in February
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Best of the West WBB power rankings: UCLA, USC remain clear No. 1 and 2 as Northern Arizona rises
item.description: The Bruins and Trojans retained their positions atop the Best of the West power rankings, with Stanford in the No. 3 spot.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Metcalfe, The Mercury News

item.title: LPGA Tour's gender-eligibility policy change draws fiery reaction from transgender golfer
item.description: Transgender golfer Hailey Davidson reacted to the LPGA Tour's stunning decision to effectively ban trans competitors in events on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flames honor Johnny Gaudreau with emotional tribute months after tragic death of NHL star and brother
item.description: The Calgary Flames hosted the Gaudreau family on Tuesday night as they paid tribute to former NHL star Johnny Gaudreau, who was killed alongside his brother in a bike accident in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LPGA and USGA to require players to be assigned female at birth or transition before male puberty
item.description: The LPGA and the USGA have updated gender policies that will require players to be assigned female at birth or to have transitioned to female before going through male puberty.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Flames honor Johnny Gaudreau, welcome his family back to Calgary in emotional pre-game tribute
item.description: The Calgary Flames paid tribute to longtime star Johnny Gaudreau on Tuesday night before their game against the Columbus Blue Jackets, months after the All-Star was killed while biking on a New Jersey road this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pro golfers call on LPGA Tour to alter gender-eligibility policy amid leadership shakeup
item.description: Pro golfers called on the LPGA Tour to alter its gender-participation policy amid a sudden leadership shakeup before the start of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jaguars sign Super Bowl champion quarterback after Trevor Lawrence injury
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars bolstered its quarterback room on Tuesday with the signing of John Wolford, the former Los Angeles Rams QB won a Super Bowl with them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wiggins, Schieffelin and Clemson's defense hold down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 win
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Chauncey Wiggins scored 12 points and Ian Schieffelin had 11 points and a career-high 20 rebounds as Clemson shut down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 victory Tuesday night in the SEC/ACC Challenge. The Tigers (8-1) held the Wildcats (7-1) to 30 points fewer than their average of 96.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cougars go cold in 83-64 loss at Providence
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team suffered its worst shooting performance of the season and lost at Providence Tuesday night, 83-64 to fall to 6-2 on the season. The Cougars shot just 33.3 percent from the field in scoring a season-low 64 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa State and SMU athletic directors in a 'stay off my lawn' moment regarding latest rankings
item.description: Athletic directors at Iowa State and SMU are in a turf war over the latest College Football Playoff rankings, leading to a “stay off my lawn" moment on social media. The background: SMU, at 11-1 and riding a nine-game winning streak into the Atlantic Coast Conference title game, was slotted at No.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Analysis: Getting benched may have been best thing that happened to Bryce Young, Anthony Richardson
item.description: On Football analyzes the biggest topics in the NFL from week to week. For more On Football analysis, head here. ___ Getting benched may have been the best thing that happened to Bryce Young and Anthony Richardson. Both second-year quarterbacks are playing well since returning to the starting lineup.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Javier Mascherano arrives at Inter Miami as coach, 5 years after team first pursued him to play
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — The first few minutes of Javier Mascherano's introductory news conference as Inter Miami coach on Tuesday were rough. He was coughing, struggling to get words out and needed a few sips of water before finally calming down. “Air conditioning in Miami,” he explained.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2024 PPR All 51 Team
item.description: 2024 PPR All 51 Team: Each season our selection committee recognizes the best players from across the county, assembling our "All 51" team. This list includes our first team picks, and honorable mentions.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: CU Buffs await bowl selection
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Colorado Buffaloes won't be heading to the Big 12 title game, but they're still sitting comfortably in the AP Top 25 at No. 20. With nine wins, including seven in conference play, it's a season most Buffs fans would call a success. Now, it's a waiting game.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KTLA 5

item.title: Utes close disappointing season with 28-14 victory
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - Utah forced three turnovers and scored two defensive touchdowns Friday night, including a 60-yard pick-6 by Zemaiah Vaughn, as the Utes snapped a seven-game losing streak with a 28-14 victory over UCF.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aggies blow 17-point fourth quarter lead in loss to CSU
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State football team could not hold a 30-13 fourth quarter lead in its season finale at Colorado State, and the Aggies end the season with a 42-37 loss to the Rams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Tennessee head coach Bill Battle dies at 82
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) -- Former University of Tennessee Coach William "Bill" Battle III died on Thanksgiving morning at the age of 82, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: 49ers to likely play Bills in heavy snow, 'dangerously cold wind chills': NWS
item.description: (KRON) -- In last week's 38-10 blowout loss to the Green Bay Packers, the 49ers' loss had little to do with the weather at Lambeau Field, also known as "The Frozen Tundra." At kickoff, it was a calm 42 degrees and mostly cloudy in the typical chilly Midwest city.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Jon Batiste, Ledisi, Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle to perform during Super Bowl pregame
item.description: The Super Bowl pregame will have some Louisiana flavor: Multi-talented performer Jon Batiste will hit the stage to sing the national anthem, while Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle are slated to perform “America the Beautiful.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boise State withdraws from Mountain West volleyball tournament rather than play San Jose State
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Boise State, which twice boycotted regular-season matches with San Jose State, pulled out of the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament Wednesday night even after securing a spot in the semifinals against the Spartans.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Caleb Williams still poised to break Bears QB records despite roller coaster rookie season
item.description: Caleb Williams has shown poise and promise as the Chicago Bears quarterback, despite the team's struggles, and is on track to break franchise records for passing yards and touchdowns.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
